t_ch: 既然「踏破不世」是一重限制条件，那么按照直觉，「三堂深处」就应该是第二重条件。
t_en: Since "worn out unearthly" is a condition, then "the three depths" should be the second condition.

t_ch: 从字面上来说，指的应该是要经过三个房间的深处。
t_en: Literally, it means going through the depths of three rooms.

t_ch: 你怎么早不开窍…
t_en: Why didn't you say this earlier...

t_ch: 嗯…也许是解开了「不世之材」，才突然福至心灵……
t_en: Well... Maybe it's the unraveling of the Unearthly Marvel that suddenly sparked my realization...

t_ch: 这么简单？
t_en: That's it?

t_ch: 应该是吧…字谜这东西就和魔术揭秘一样，一旦知道了谜底，就会觉得是对自己智识的侮辱，但这只是马后炮的错觉罢了……
t_en: I think so... Solving riddles is like solving magic tricks. Once you know the answer, you would think it's an insult to your intelligence, but that's just the illusion of hindsight.

t_ch: 有点头绪了。
t_en: It's all coming together now.

t_ch: 所以如果密信揭露的是事实，那么水魈就藏在一个既有不世之材，又要经过三个房间的地方……
t_en: So if the letter is correct, the Xenohydro should be hiding in a place where there's Unearthly Marvel, but also you have to pass through three rooms to get there.

t_ch: 唉，如果{NICKNAME}还是坚持不肯放弃的话，那我就帮你帮到底吧。
t_en: *sigh* If you still insist on continuing, I'll help you to the end.

t_ch: 这一次，一定不会再失手了。
t_en: This time, we won't fail.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你看，经过「不世之材」修复的道路，进入三个房间联通的最深处……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, look, after passing through the road that was repaired with Unearthly Marvel, we entered the deepest part where the three rooms are connected...

t_ch: 这就是所谓「三堂深处」…快进来吧！
t_en: This is the so-called "the three depths"... Come on in!

t_ch: 终于抓到你了，{NICKNAME}…或者我应该叫你，水魈才对呢？
t_en: I finally got you, {NICKNAME}... Or should I call you... Xenohydro!

t_ch: 还好我早有防备，早在几天前，{NICKNAME}就找到我，提醒我注意近期或有水魈流窜，没想到这么快你就送上门了。
t_en: Good thing I was on guard. As early as a few days ago, the real {NICKNAME} had already warned me that there may be a Xenohydro wreaking havoc in the near future. I didn't expect you to come so soon.

t_ch: 让我想想，把你干掉，绕过卡波特先生，交给艾丝妲小姐，这样的话，我就不用再当流水工了……
t_en: Let me see, I can just take care of you, bypass Mr. Capote and hand you over to Miss Asta, so I don't have to be a slave anymore...

t_ch: 你在说什么？
t_en: What are you talking about?

t_ch: 我在说，绕了这么一大圈，我和{NICKNAME}终于抓到了你——贼喊捉贼的「异星水魈」！
t_en: I'm saying that after all that, the real {NICKNAME} and I finally caught you, Xenohydro!

t_ch: 我不是水魈！
t_en: I'm not the Xenohydro!

t_ch: 终于抓到了你，贼喊捉贼的「异星水魈」。如果你不是，那倒是请你看看，站在那边的又是谁……
t_en: I've caught you, Xenohydro! If you are not Xenohydro, then who that is standing over there?

t_ch: 早该猜到了…
t_en: I should have guessed...

t_ch: 终于抓到了你，贼喊捉贼的「异星水魈」。废话少说，要是不服气，尽管去和{NICKNAME}对峙吧。
t_en: I caught you, Xenohydro! Enough nonsense. If you're not convinced, sort it out with {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 终于抓到你了，「异星水魈」，这次你可别想逃。
t_en: I finally caught you, Xenohydro. You won't get away this time.

t_ch: 你才是水魈。
t_en: You're the Xenohydro.

t_ch: 我可不这么认为。你看，我们都是两只眼睛一个嘴巴，谈吐无异，气度相像。
t_en: I don't think so. You see, we both have two eyes and a mouth. We sound the same and act the same way.

t_ch: 考虑到现在没有第四个人在场，拉明娜认为谁是水魈，谁就是水魈。
t_en: Since there is no fourth person present, whoever Lamina thinks is the Xenohydro, is the Xenohydro.

t_ch: 为什么冒充我？
t_en: Why impersonate me?

t_ch: 这话我应该问你才对，我堂堂{NICKNAME}，「星穹列车」的正义成员，行走的星核容器……
t_en: I should be asking you that. I'm {NICKNAME}! The official crew member of the Astral Express, the walking Stellaron vessel...

t_ch: 力压「反物质军团」的无敌强手，银河驰名的冉冉新星，岂是你这水魈可以冒充的？
t_en: The invincible conqueror of the Antimatter Legion, the infamous rising star of the cosmos. Who do you think you are impersonating, Xenohydro?

t_ch: 你顶着我的脸，是不是为了享受我的好名声，方便在空间站内到处招摇撞骗？
t_en: Aren't you using my face to take advantage of my reputation so you can conduct scams in the space station?

t_ch: 空间站的人会相信我。
t_en: People in the space station will believe me.

t_ch: 相信？多么廉价的词语。别说其他人，现在连拉明娜也不信你。
t_en: Believe? What a joke. Not even Lamina believes you right now, let alone the others.

t_ch: 我早就抢先一步提醒她，最近可能会有人冒充我找她套近乎，你说你不是水魈，有什么证据吗？
t_en: I warned her earlier that someone might pretend to be me to get close to her. Do you have any proof that you are not the Xenohydro?

t_ch: 我有棒球棍。
t_en: I have a baseball bat.

t_ch: 棒球棍，那是什么？能够定义你究竟是谁的本体？还是全宇宙只此无二的神器？
t_en: Baseball bat... what's that? Can you use it to prove your identity? Or is it the only one of its kind in the universe?

t_ch: 是我的本体。
t_en: It's my body.

t_ch: 嗯？你的本体不该是星核吗？竟然是一种外置的东西吗？
t_en: Huh? Isn't your body the Stellaron? So it's an external object?

t_ch: 是一种神器。
t_en: It's an artifact.

t_ch: 你是星核容器也就罢了，竟然还有这样的宝贝，怎么跟之前空间站里那些拿了剧本的人似的，真够威风的……
t_en: I get it, you're the stellaron vessel, fine, but you even have this bad boy? Hey, it's like those guys with the scripts in the space station before. It's pretty cool.

t_ch: 我能打爆你。
t_en: I can bash you to a pulp.

t_ch: 你是这么暴躁的人吗？君子动口不动手，这张俏脸要是挂了彩，多难看呀。
t_en: Is that the kind of violent thug you are? Hey, you don't want to ruin this pretty face, do you?

t_ch: 我劝你还是放下屠刀，乖乖就范，收容舱的日子也没那么糟，上次，那个温命得队长差点就进去了，你俩今后也许还能搭个伴。
t_en: I advise you to lay down your arms and hand yourself over. Life in the Storage Zone is not so bad. Last time, Mr. Wen Mingy almost went in. You two may keep each other company in the future.

t_ch: 我和她是伙伴。
t_en: She and I are partners.

t_ch: 嗯，想把赌注押在第三方证人的判断之上……那拉明娜，就由你来定生死吧。
t_en: Yeah, let's see what the third-party witness says... Lamina, it's your call.

t_ch: 拉明娜？你怎么又不说话了？！你不会这时候良心发现，想反水吧，咕噜噜。
t_en: Lamina? Why aren't you saying anything!? Did you suddenly find your conscience? You want to back out now? *gurgle*

t_ch: 你已经暴露了。
t_en: You just got yourself exposed.

t_ch: 咕噜噜，暴露了又怎样？{NICKNAME}，我想你还没有搞清楚现在的状况。
t_en: *gurgle* So what? {NICKNAME}, I don't think you really understand the situation.

t_ch: 现在是什么状况？
t_en: What's the situation?

t_ch: 咕噜噜，还真是，你怎么跟拉明娜一样不争气？！那么我来解释解释。
t_en: *gurgle* For crying out loud. Are you two serious!? Do I have to spell it out for you?

t_ch: 你可别小看人。
t_en: Don't underestimate me.

t_ch: 那你说说，现在是什么状况？
t_en: Oh... well fine. Then tell me, what's going on now?

t_ch: 什么状况还用我说吗？
t_en: Do I have to spell it out?

t_ch: 别在这里跟我打哑谜！我看你呀，就是没搞清楚状况。
t_en: Don't play games with me! I don't think you understand the situation.

t_ch: 那个共犯就是拉明娜。
t_en: Lamina is the accomplice.

t_ch: 没错！不愧是我们，{NICKNAME}！但是你只猜到了前半段。
t_en: That's right! Good one, {NICKNAME}! But you only guessed the first half of our plan.

t_ch: 你们想拿我狸猫换太子。
t_en: You want me to take your place as the Xenohydro.

t_ch: 再正确不过！不愧是我们，{NICKNAME}！竟然把一切都看穿了。
t_en: That's right! Well done, {NICKNAME}! You saw through everything.

t_ch: 我早就猜到有人会来调查我的行踪，所以我和拉明娜，决定把多管闲事的人骗到这里，把他干掉，冒充水魈交上去，从此一劳永逸。
t_en: I had long guessed that someone would come to investigate my whereabouts, so Lamina and I decided to lure the investigator here, kill them and then hand them in as the Xenohydro, then we'll finally be free.

t_ch: 只是没想到来的是你，拉明娜心慈手软，想要给你一个机会，计划才从「武力清除」变成了「循循善诱」——
t_en: But I didn't expect you to come. Lamina was soft. She wanted to give you a chance. And so we changed the plan from "elimination" to "persuasion"—

t_ch: 现在你想离开这里，只有两个选择，要么乖乖答应，代替我去蹲收容舱；要么被我干掉后，再代替我去蹲收容舱。
t_en: Now if you want to leave this place, you only have two choices. Either obey and go to Storage Zone in place of me, or die and then go to Storage Zone instead of me.

t_ch: 拉明娜为什么帮你？
t_en: Why did Lamina help you?

t_ch: 都这时候了你还在想为什么！作为你的对手，我觉得我受到了侮辱，咕噜噜，我要给你点颜色瞧瞧！
t_en: Jeez, is that really what you're worried about? At a time like this? You know what? As your opponent, I feel insulted. *gurgle* I'm gonna teach you a lesson!

t_ch: 我还有第三个选择。
t_en: I have a third option.

t_ch: 那是什么？难道是把我干掉…咕噜噜，你好大的口气！我要给你点颜色瞧瞧！
t_en: What's that? To kill me? *gurgle* You talk big! I'm gonna teach you a lesson!

t_ch: 看来，我和你，注定要共沉沦了……
t_en: It seems you and I are destined to go down together...

t_ch: 对不起，{NICKNAME}…我就是那个与水魈合谋的共犯，我愿意接受处罚。
t_en: Sorry, {NICKNAME}... I'm the accomplice in collusion with the Xenohydro. I'm willing to accept my punishment.

t_ch: 这到底是怎么一回事？
t_en: What on earth is going on?

t_ch: 你是知道我的，知道拉明娜究竟是怎样一个人。
t_en: You know me. You know what kind of person Lamina is.

t_ch: 没有天赋。没有作品。只有靠多做杂活，才能在夹缝中证明自己的价值。
t_en: No talent. No achievements. I can barely prove my value by doing the most trivial jobs.

t_ch: 但一辈子做流水工，对得起我自己，却对不起我的家人。
t_en: But working on the assembly line all my life... It's fine for me, but how can I face my family?

t_ch: 亲人牺牲了自己的一切，供养我来这里，我难道就用…捡垃圾，来报答吗？
t_en: They have sacrificed everything for me so that I can come here. Do I repay them... by being a cleaner?

t_ch: 我不能，也没脸这么做。卡波特是第一个跳板，唯一的跳板。虽然要依附于他，但那总归是隶属于界种科辖下的，一个体面的工作。
t_en: I can't, and I am not shameless enough to do that. Capote was my first springboard to success, the only springboard. Although I had to rely on him, it's still a decent job in the Department of Ecology.

t_ch: 我一度以为只要好好帮他做事，至少还有机会熬出头。没有功劳，硬熬总行吧？
t_en: I once thought that if I just served him well, I would at least get a chance to succeed. Hard work always pays off, right?

t_ch: ——直到水魈冒充了我，它以捉弄卡波特为乐，处处斗智斗勇。
t_en: Until the Xenohydro impersonated me to prank Capote and make a mockery of him.

t_ch: 它就像一面镜子，照出了我无所遁形的丑态。是那么卑微。那么弱小。那么…无力。
t_en: It's like a mirror, it showed me my pitiful appearance, so humble, so weak, so... powerless.

t_ch: 它也像一面宝鉴，照出的拉明娜，能够摆脱他人的钳制，终于活得像个人样。
t_en: It also taught me a valuable lesson. Even Lamina can liberate herself from the constraints of others and live like a person.

t_ch: 我第一次发现，我愿意用平庸的前途，来换恣意潇洒的生活。
t_en: For the first time, I found that I was willing to trade my mediocre future for a free and unrestrained life.

t_ch: 于是我放任了这一切。直到科员窝藏水魈的谣言风传一时，他们叫嚣要严惩知情不报之人……
t_en: So I let it all happen, until the rumor that someone in the staff harbored the Xenohydro became widespread. They called for severe punishment for those who did not report relevant information...

t_ch: 我才发现，我个人的愿望，又哪里重要呢？我可以做个劣下的科员，但黑塔不能赶走我。
t_en: That's when I realized that my personal wishes were not important. I can just be a low-level researcher, but I can't let the space station drive me out.

t_ch: 不能把家里对我的最后期望也毁掉。
t_en: I can't ruin my family's last bit of expectation for me.

t_ch: 当听说有人开始调查水魈与共犯一事，我就下定决心，要铲除对手，保护自己。
t_en: When I heard that someone began investigating the Xenohydro and its accomplice, I made up my mind to eradicate my opponent and protect myself.

t_ch: 好不容易决心勇敢一次，却没想到，被你我的牵绊干扰了判断。如果来的是别人，那该多好啊。
t_en: Finally, I decided to be brave for once, but I didn't expect to be hampered by the bond between us. If only it was someone else who came here.

t_ch: 我能理解你的苦衷。
t_en: I can understand why you did it.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。但我说这些并不是为了换取你的原谅。只是…把你当作朋友，久违地，说一说心里话而已。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. But I'm not saying this to get your forgiveness. I was just... talking to you as a friend, getting things off my chest.

t_ch: 你的勇敢用错了方向。
t_en: Your bravery is wasted in the wrong pursuit.

t_ch: 是吗？也许吧。当人被逼到绝路上时，急智是会显得冷酷绝情。但那只是出于生存的本能。
t_en: Is it? Maybe. When a person is pushed to a dead-end, wit might appear cold and heartless. But it's just survival instinct.

t_ch: 你说了很多不拉明娜的话。
t_en: You said a lot of things that are unlike the Lamina I know.

t_ch: 或许是平时过分压抑了自己。眼睛总是盯着别人的需求，才会忘了低头看看自己的心。
t_en: Maybe it's because I suppress myself too hard. I am always focused on other people's needs and have forgotten to look inside myself.

t_ch: 如果我能早一点从别人眼中看到自己的模样，遭遇也许就会截然不同，不论是依靠水魈，还是依靠那封信。
t_en: If I could see myself in other people's eyes earlier, my life might be different. If it wasn't for the Xenohydro, then it was the letter.

t_ch: 我早就看出来了，那封信中所写的共犯就是我。只是我真的…不敢面对。
t_en: I had already figured that the "accomplice" in that letter was referring to me. It's just that I didn't really... have the courage to face it.

t_ch: 其实，一切都写在了信里。
t_en: In fact, everything is written in the letter.

t_ch: 我也早就看出来，那封信中所写的共犯就是我。只是我真的…不敢面对。
t_en: I had already figured that the "accomplice" in that letter was referring to me. It's just that I didn't really... have the courage to face it.

t_ch: 戚戚与之同谋…
t_en: Better off alone...

t_ch: 不如皎皎独立。
t_en: Than to be an accomplice.

t_ch: 他日纵然回首…
t_en: One day, as they turn around...

t_ch: 自有星辉拂路。
t_en: A path will be lit by the radiance of the stars.

t_ch: 「戚戚与之同谋，不如皎皎独立。他日纵然回首，自有星辉拂路。」
t_en: "Better off alone than to be an accomplice. One day, as they turn around, a path will be lit by the radiance of the stars."

t_ch: 之前完全忽略了这些话啊。难道这是一封…劝慰信？
t_en: I completely ignored these words earlier. Is this letter... meant to persuade me?

t_ch: 告密人打从一开始就在劝说我，让我放弃与水魈合谋，独立去过自己的生活？
t_en: So from the beginning, the informant tried to persuade me to give up my conspiracy with the Xenohydro and live my own life independently...

t_ch: 或者说，独立的意义，不仅是要摆脱水魈，还包括卡波特，以及我的软弱。
t_en: In other words, independence does not only mean leaving the Xenohydro, but also Capote and my weaknesses.

t_ch: 那么我明白了。虽然明白的时机，有些晚了。
t_en: Now I understand. Although this realization has come a little too late.

t_ch: 犯了错就应受到惩罚。
t_en: You've committed a crime. You must be punished.

t_ch: 我明白，我会跟你回去，接受应有的处罚。
t_en: I understand. I'll go back with you and accept due punishment.

t_ch: 但是在那之前，我会向你证明，经历了这番曲折，我已经做出了改变。
t_en: But before that, I will prove to you that I have really changed because of this experience.

t_ch: 我可以放过你和水魈。
t_en: I can let you and the Xenohydro go.

t_ch: 真的吗？谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。我会严加看管水魈，不再让他危害空间站。
t_en: Really? Thank you, {NICKNAME}. I will keep a closer eye on the Xenohydro and stop him from endangering the space station.

t_ch: 而且，我会向你证明，经历了这番曲折，我已经做出了改变。
t_en: I will also prove to you that I have really changed because of this experience.

t_ch: 你还有机会，但水魈必须回收。
t_en: You still have a chance, but the Xenohydro must be returned.

t_ch: 你的意思是，你不会告诉别人共犯究竟是谁？…谢谢你，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: You mean, you won't tell them who the accomplice was? Thank you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我会珍惜你给我的这次机会，并且证明，经历了这番曲折，我已经做出了改变。
t_en: I will cherish the opportunity you gave me and I will also prove to you that I have really changed because of this experience.

t_ch: 你回来啦，有结论了么？
t_en: You're back. Did you conclude the matter?

t_ch: 水魈抓回来了…
t_en: I got the Xenohydro back...

t_ch: 对不起，没抓到…
t_en: Sorry, I couldn't catch them...

t_ch: 嗯，不急，水魈倒是次要的事…那边和卡波特站在一起的，是拉明娜吧？
t_en: Well, don't worry, the Xenohydro is of secondary importance... Is that Lamina standing with Capote over there?

t_ch: 你这个逢场作戏的骗子，你践踏了我们的真心。
t_en: You fraud. You trampled on our sincerity.

t_ch: 曾经，波少是那么完美，现在也没有逃过塌房的命运……
t_en: Oh, Master Capote used to be so perfect, and now he is just another deceitful celebrity...

t_ch: 波少！你竟然喜欢吃蛋黄酱那么难吃的东西，把我送你的礼物还给我！
t_en: Master Capote! I can't believe you eat mayonnaise. That's disgusting! Give me back the gifts I gave you!

t_ch: 拉明娜，你叫这么多人来干什么？
t_en: Lamina, why did you invite all these people?

t_ch: 不是我叫他们过来的。我只是把你的粉丝回信都是由我代笔的事实告诉了她们。
t_en: I didn't invite them here. I just told them the fact that I was the one who replied to all your fans.

t_ch: 大家别急，晚点听我解释…拉明娜，你是一点也不想要我给你的好处了是吧……
t_en: Everybody calm down, let me explain later... Lamina, you don't want the benefits I give you at all anymore, do you?

t_ch: 对，我不想要。从今以后，我不会再帮你做饭、洗衣擦鞋、代笔写信，也不会再做你的傀儡，听明白了吗？
t_en: That's right, I don't want any of it. From now on, I won't help you cook, wash your clothes and shoes, write your letters, or be your puppet. Get it?

t_ch: 如果你还有事找我，记得去找我的秘书「不存在先生」拿号。
t_en: If you want to find me, remember to ask my secretary Mr. Nonexistent for a number.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你怎么看待这件事？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, what do you think of this?

t_ch: 不知道他们在吵什么？
t_en: I don't know what they are arguing about.

t_ch: 真没想到你口风这么严哪。不过现在，我们大可以打开天窗说亮话。
t_en: I didn't think you can be so tight-lipped, but now, we should be honest and clear this up.

t_ch: 她已经能够独当一面。
t_en: She'll be able to look after herself.

t_ch: 没错，这都多亏了你从中斡旋，才能既保全她的安危，又促成她的心境转变。
t_en: Yes, thanks to your mediation. Not only did you ensure her safety, but also encouraged her to change.

t_ch: 你好像知道背后的事…
t_en: You seem to know the back story.

t_ch: 我自认为策划得非常缜密，没想到还是被你察觉了。
t_en: I thought I was careful, but you still noticed.

t_ch: …哎呀，想起「好似凌霄攀援，难免低入尘埃」这句，平仄、韵脚都没押上，我下的功夫还是不够嘛。
t_en: ...Oops, "Like the risk of falling into the depths when trying to mount the sky." I didn't use any rhymes or enough alliteration for this line. It's still not good enough.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，不瞒你说，宓堂空境解语人不是别人，就是我。当初要写这封古言信，我可是向温明德队长请教了十来天，吃了不少苦头呢。
t_en: Hehe, to tell you the truth, the Cryptologist From Locus Silentii is none other than yours truly. This letter took me more than ten days to write, and I asked Mr. Wen for advice. It wasn't easy.

t_ch: 写好这封信之后，我很长一段时间都找不到合适的人，来转交劝慰信。还好水魈从来只是小打小闹，回收它也不必急于一时。
t_en: After I wrote it, I couldn't find the right person to get it delivered. Fortunately, the Xenohydro is just a prankster, and there's no rush to retrieve it.

t_ch: 后来{NICKNAME}出现，不啻为最好的人选。我急中生智，想着让你和拉明娜共同经历一番，也许能有意想不到的收获。
t_en: Then you appeared, {NICKNAME}. And you turned out to be the best candidate. So I came up with the idea to let you and Lamina share some experiences together, see where it takes you.

t_ch: 毕竟经过这次事件你也应该了解，拉明娜是个善良的女孩，她值得再拥有一次机会。
t_en: But now you should also understand that Lamina is actually a kindhearted girl and she deserves another chance.

t_ch: 你也是个温柔的人…
t_en: You are also very kind.

t_ch: 她一定会改过自新。
t_en: She is bound to turn over a new leaf.

t_ch: 何苦绕这么大圈子。
t_en: Why go around this massive circle?

t_ch: 好啦，既然我的初衷已经达成，我宣布，异星水魈案就此告一段落，这是{NICKNAME}来回帮忙的奖赏。
t_en: Well, now that my objective has been achieved, I announce the Xenohydro case is officially over. Here is your reward. Thanks for all your help.

t_ch: 今后还请多回黑塔来看看。别忘了，这里始终有你倾力建设的痕迹，还有你曾倾心帮助过的那些人。
t_en: Please come back to the space station more in the future. Don't forget, there are a lot of people who've received your help here, and many traces of your hard work throughout the place.

t_ch: 欸，你说这卡波特是不是彻底玩儿完啦？以后，还会有粉丝把他当偶像吗？
t_en: Hey, do you think Capote is over? Will the fans still idolize him?

t_ch: 要我说，就是这空间站里帅哥太少了，个个儿才把他捧上天去。
t_en: If you ask me, there are just not enough good-looking guys in the station. That's why they treat him like a god around here.

t_ch: 咕噜噜……
t_en: *gurgle*...

t_ch: 你是？！
t_en: It's you?

t_ch: 我可比他帅。
t_en: I'm better looking than him.

t_ch: 你都比他帅。
t_en: You ARE better looking than him.

t_ch: 咕噜噜，我从卡波特那里偷了些好东西，送给{NICKNAME}。
t_en: *gurgle* I stole some good stuff from Capote. They're for you, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 毕竟，咱们可有一段时光是「不分你我」哪，怎么能亏了你？
t_en: After all, we used to be inseparable. How can I not look after you?

t_ch: 拿好拿好，后会无期~
t_en: Take it. See you later.

t_ch: 不好意思，各位粉丝，你们别听那女人乱讲…大家不要聚集在此处，以免干扰研究秩序，引来防卫科的大哥们。
t_en: Sorry, everybody, don't listen to that woman... Let's not gather here. We don't want to disturb the researchers and attract any unnecessary attention from security.

t_ch: 空间站里谁不知道拉明娜是你波少最信任、最得力的秘书。她说的话，那一定不会假！
t_en: Everyone in the space station knows that Lamina is your most trusted secretary. What she said must be true!

t_ch: 拉明娜某种程度上可是我们的粉丝头头啊，连她都离开你了，那不是说明，你根本不值得……
t_en: Lamina is the leader of your fanclub to some extent. If even she has left you, then maybe you're really not worth it...

t_ch: 算了，在这里呆再久也没用。能讨回真心吗？能讨回我们的笑和泪吗？能讨回在他身上寄寓的研究梦想吗？！
t_en: Forget it, there's no point staying here any longer. Can we get our hearts back? Can we get back our laughter and tears? Can we get back our hopes and dreams!?

t_ch: 我看，还是各自回去，各自疗伤吧……
t_en: I think we should go back and let our hearts heal.

t_ch: 这位叫做…呃的科员说得对！各位先请回吧。
t_en: Miss... uh... researcher is right! Please go back for now.

t_ch: 她的名字叫做…呃？
t_en: Her name is... uh?

t_ch: 呵，都看见了？他连我们姓甚名谁也不知道呢。
t_en: Oh, see? He doesn't even know our names.

t_ch: ——所以你叫什么名字？一会儿找个地方一起喝点「卡利百」？我现在可是一肚子火！
t_en: So what's your name? Why don't we find a place to drink Kali-Kapo later? I need to vent!

t_ch: 行、行啊，可我怕我一开口，这眼泪就止不住……
t_en: Okay... Okay, but I'm afraid I won't be able to stop my tears as soon as I start to speak...

t_ch: 你们都不用工作的吗？
t_en: Don't you all have to work?

t_ch: 哪、哪还有心思干活啊，呜……
t_en: How can I be in the mood to work? Woo...

t_ch: 你在问我吗？今天，的确是没有什么研究的活啦。
t_en: Are you asking me? There is really not much research work today.

t_ch: 老实说，「波少」真是叫我失望啊。亏我有什么应物科的好东西都想着他。现在看来，还不如送给……
t_en: To be honest, Master Capote really disappointed me. Can't believe I wanted to give all the good things in the Department of Implement Arts. Now it seems we might as well give them all to...

t_ch: 没意思，还是去找小阿德勒玩吧。
t_en: Nah. I should just go play with little Adler.

t_ch: 这个教训你好好记住吧。
t_en: Learn from this lesson.

t_ch: 你又是什么人…少多管闲事……
t_en: Who are you anyway... Mind your own business...

t_ch: 呵，他连我们的名字都记不住，要他记住教训，恐怕也是够呛的。
t_en: Oh, he can't even remember our names. I'm afraid it's hard for him to remember this lesson.

t_ch: 啊？那「波少」岂不是连收了我什么礼物也记不得了？不行不行，我一定得要回去，不然被应物科的伯伯们发现了，那就糟糕了。
t_en: Oh? Can't Master Capote even remember what gift he got from me? Oh no, I have to get back. If those old guys in Implement Arts catch me, it'll get ugly.

t_ch: 各位好姐姐！听我的，先散了吧。
t_en: My ladies! Listen to me, please just go now.

t_ch: 那个，后续我会送上慰问信，来安抚大家受惊的心灵。放心，那一定是由我本人亲笔撰写的慰问信。
t_en: Well, I'll send you all a letter of condolence later to mend your hearts. Don't worry, it will be written by myself personally.

t_ch: 嘁，谁稀罕呵。光是处理你以前那些代笔信，就不知道要花多长时间。
t_en: Urgh, who wants that. It will take me forever just to deal with your previous ghostwriter letters.

t_ch: 我的那份还是给我吧，刚好凑个整……
t_en: Oh fine, just give me one. It'll complete the collection...

t_ch: 那边看热闹的，也都散了吧！
t_en: Hey everybody! Nothing to see here, let's bounce!

t_ch: 也不知道拉明娜怎么突然魔怔了，别是有共犯，或者幕后主使者吧？
t_en: I don't know why Lamina threw that tantrum all of a sudden. There must be an accomplice or someone behind the scenes.

t_ch: 唉，没时间胡思乱想，这次一大堆的慰问信，还等着我自己写呢……
t_en: Alas, I don't have time to think about it. I still have a lot of consolation letters to write.

t_ch: 拜托拜托，我只是想站在这儿看个热闹就走，千万别被其他人发现……
t_en: Sorry, sorry, I am just here to see the excitement. Don't let the others see me...

t_ch: 欸，是你啊！好险好险，幸好来的不是防卫科的人。
t_en: Hey, it's you! Close call! Luckily you're not from security.

t_ch: 哈！瞧，他们果然没跟进来！自由啦！
t_en: Hah! See, they weren't brave enough to follow us! Freedom!

t_ch: 活该！哈哈，想逮住我们，还早了几百年哪！
t_en: Serves them right! Better luck next time, slowpokes!

t_ch: 太冒险了。
t_en: That was too risky.

t_ch: 结果就是一切～敢冒风险的人才能获得成功～
t_en: Oh please, it's the result that counts~ Gotta take risks sometimes if you wanna succeed~

t_ch: 干得漂亮！
t_en: Great work!

t_ch: 嗨，功劳是大家的～
t_en: Hehe, it was a team effort~

t_ch: 运气好罢了。
t_en: We got lucky.

t_ch: 运气也是实力的一部分～
t_en: Well, you can get good at getting lucky~

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫只是被我们打了个出其不意，用不了多久，他们一定会追进来的。
t_en: We caught them by surprise, nothing more. They'll be in pursuit soon enough.

t_ch: 沿着道路移动吧，先确保安全，再作下一步计划。
t_en: Let's follow the path. We need to guarantee our own safety before making any further plans.

t_ch: 这道门，好像没法直接打开啊…欸，你们看这个机关的构造，是不是有点眼熟？
t_en: Doesn't seem like there's any way to open this gate... Ooh, check this mechanism out. Look familiar?

t_ch: 的确，在希露瓦的机械屋那见过。
t_en: Yes, we saw one in Serval's workshop.

t_ch: 得抓紧时间破解，追兵就在我们身后。
t_en: We need to solve it before we run out of time. The Guards are right on our heels.

t_ch: 成功了，开了开了！快走吧，我好像都听见追兵的脚步声了！
t_en: We did it! It's open! Quick, let's go, I think I heard footsteps!

t_ch: 这道门没有开关耶。
t_en: I don't see a switch for the gate.

t_ch: 啊，好像在那儿！
t_en: Ah! Over there!

t_ch: 有个怪物在开关附近打转，得先解决它。
t_en: There's a monster in the area. We'll need to deal with it first.

t_ch: 就算遭到了裂界的侵蚀，这里始终是贝洛伯格的一部分，是我们曾经的家园。铁卫对这里了若指掌。
t_en: Even though it has suffered Fragmentum corrosion, this is still part of Belobog. It was our home. The Guards know this place like the back of their hand.

t_ch: 逃亡游戏结束了，放下武器，跟我回去吧。
t_en: Enough cat and mouse — drop your weapons and come with me.

t_ch: 你真是死缠烂打啊…搞不懂我们到底犯了什么罪，让你一直追到这儿都不罢手！
t_en: Ugh, you're such a nuisance! Just what crime are we supposed to have committed again? It better be worth you following us all the way out here!

t_ch: 我接收的命令是捉拿你们，至于详细的罪责和判罚，裁判团会向你们解释。
t_en: My orders are to arrest you. It is for the adjudication panel to present you with the nature and penalty of your alleged crimes.

t_ch: 你昨天见过我们，还记得吗？那时我们是作为可可利亚女士的贵客被招待的。
t_en: You saw us yesterday, do you remember? Madam Cocolia received us as honored guests.

t_ch: 一夜之间这翻天覆地的变化，我们也很难接受。
t_en: How can such a drastic change have occurred in the course of one night?

t_ch: …守护者大人调查了你们的背景。
t_en: ...The Madam Guardian investigated your backgrounds.

t_ch: 她在昨晚召见我，说你们欺骗了她；你们的身份和目的皆是伪造的，意在破坏筑城者对贝洛伯格的掌控。
t_en: She summoned me last night to tell me that you had deceived her. Your identities and purpose here are counterfeit. You seek to overthrow the rule of the Architects.

t_ch: 啊，这老巫婆怎么人前一套人后一套！
t_en: Ah! What a two-faced hag!

t_ch: 公开侮辱大守护者只会让你们罪加一等，放下武器投降吧！
t_en: Publicly insulting the supreme guardian only elevates the seriousness of your crimes. Throw down your arms and surrender!

t_ch: 多言无益了，三月。至少有一件事很清楚：现在我们绝不能被逮捕。
t_en: We're wasting our words, March. At least one thing is clear: We mustn't be caught.

t_ch: 既然没路可逃了，那就让他们见识下{RUBY_B#星穹列车}列车组{RUBY_E#}的厉害！
t_en: Well, if there's no escape, then maybe it's time we gave them a taste of Astral Express medicine!

t_ch: 他们的阵型…完全找不到破绽啊！
t_en: Ugh — can't find a way to break their formation!

t_ch: 冷静下来，逐个击破吧。
t_en: Regroup! Let's divide and conquer.

t_ch: 这孩子…有点过于厉害了啊。喂，丹恒，快把你隐藏的力量用出来呀！
t_en: This girl's pretty strong... Hey Dan Heng, maybe now's a good time to show off your secret strength!?

t_ch: …你先吧。
t_en: ...You first.

t_ch: 嘁，没意思。
t_en: Ugh, you're no fun.

t_ch: 束手就擒吧，入侵者！我会确保你们得到公正的审判。
t_en: Intruders, give it up! You will be guaranteed a fair trial.

t_ch: 呃，我不是故意要破坏这个紧张的气氛哈～
t_en: Ooh! Sorry, I didn't mean to ruin the intensity of the moment~

t_ch: 到底发生了什么事，为什么那个大守护者突然派人来抓我们啊？
t_en: So what happened back there? Why did the supreme guardian send people to arrest us?

t_ch: 在人家睡得正香的时候搞突然袭击，也太卑鄙了吧！
t_en: Imagine launching a surprise attack when your enemy is fast asleep. The cheek!

t_ch: 那个女人身上有种令人不安的气息…直觉果然不会撒谎。
t_en: That woman had a troubling aura... our instincts were correct.

t_ch: 等一下！里面有银鬃铁卫，小心别惊动他们！
t_en: Wait! There are Silvermane Guards here. Careful not to alert them!

t_ch: 八成是从其他方向前来围堵我们的士兵。
t_en: They must have entered from another direction to cut us off.

t_ch: 不要正面跟他们冲突，找找别的路吧。
t_en: We should avoid an open conflict. Let's find another path.

t_ch: 该怎么做才能走出去……
t_en: How can I get out of here...

t_ch: 小朋友，你迷路了吗？
t_en: Hey, are you lost?

t_ch: …迷路？
t_en: ...Lost?

t_ch: 我没有迷路，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不用担心。他说过，只要听他的话，任何坏事都不会发生。
t_en: I'm not lost, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. Don't worry about me! He said nothing bad can ever happen so long as I listen to him.

t_ch: 你一个人在这？太危险了。
t_en: What are you doing here all by yourself? It's not safe.

t_ch: …危险？
t_en: ...Not safe?

t_ch: 这里没有危险，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不用担心。他说过，只要听她的话，任何坏事都不会发生。
t_en: It's plenty safe here, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. Don't worry about me! He said nothing bad can ever happen so long as I listen to him.

t_ch: 「他」是谁？你的爸爸吗？
t_en: Who's "he"? Your dad?

t_ch: 爸爸？不对，不是。他是重要的人…但不是爸爸。
t_en: Dad? No, nope. He's important, but he's not my dad...

t_ch: 「他」在说胡话？这是裂界…
t_en: Is "he" talking nonsense? We are in the Fragmentum...

t_ch: 裂界…？不对，这里是商店街。他说过，这里是贝洛伯格最热闹的地方。
t_en: Fragmentum...? No, this is our shopping street. He said this is the busiest spot in all of Belobog.

t_ch: 对了…他说过，礼貌的孩子不能忘了自我介绍。我叫希莉儿，很开心…很荣幸认识你。
t_en: Oh, he also mentioned that it's polite to always introduce yourself. So, my name is Cyrille, and it's nice... an honor, I mean, to meet you.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，跟我走吧。
t_en: {NICKNAME}. come with me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}…很神奇的名字。
t_en: {NICKNAME}... What a strange name.

t_ch: 对不起，这么说不太礼貌。他说过…对任何人都要时刻保持礼貌。
t_en: Sorry, it's not polite to say that. He said... gotta be polite, to everyone, at all times.

t_ch: 我得带你离开这里，希莉儿。
t_en: I have to get you out of here, Cyrille.

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不愿意告诉我你的名字吗？
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}, don't you want to tell me your name?

t_ch: 嗯…没关系，别在意。他说过…对任何人都要时刻保持礼貌。
t_en: Oh... that's okay. Don't worry. He said... gotta be polite, to everyone, at all times.

t_ch: 我不能跟{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}走。我必须找到走出去的方法才行。
t_en: I can't go with you, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. I have to find a way to get out.

t_ch: 你到底在说什么…我听不懂。
t_en: What are you saying... I don't understand.

t_ch: 对不起。希莉儿不是姐姐…姐姐很聪明，一定能跟你解释清楚。
t_en: I'm sorry. I'm not like my sister... My sister is smart, and can explain everything to you.

t_ch: 良心不允许我把你留在这儿。
t_en: I can't just leave a little kid like you here all alone.

t_ch: 别担心。哪怕他不在，还有姐姐在。姐姐也会保护希莉儿。
t_en: Don't worry. Even if he's not there, my sister is with me. She'll take care of me.

t_ch: 得找到走出去的办法才行…我到底是哪一步没走对呢？
t_en: I have to find a way to get out... Where on earth did I go wrong?

t_ch: （这个叫希莉儿的小女孩，好像一直在盯着那边看……）
t_en: (This little girl named Cyrille seems to be fixated on that spot the whole time...)

t_ch: （莫非她所谓的「走出去」，和这些箱子有关系？）
t_en: (Could it be that her idea of "getting out" is connected to these boxes?)

t_ch: 我来帮你处理这些箱子吧。
t_en: I'll help you sort out these boxes.

t_ch: 可以吗？但这是我自己的事，不应该麻烦别人……
t_en: For real? That'd be great! But I shouldn't bother others with my own business...

t_ch: 嗯…但我已经在这里待了很久很久了。如果有人帮忙的话，也许就能走出去……
t_en: Mmm... but I've been here for such a long, long, long time. Maybe if someone helps me, I can get out...

t_ch: 如果{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}你愿意的话…可以帮帮我吗？
t_en: If it won't be a bother to you, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}... Could you help me out?

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}…真厉害！我在这里待了很久，但一直没想到办法……
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}... you are amazing! I've been here for so long but never could figure out how to solve this...

t_ch: 可是…为什么？为什么希莉儿还是走不出去……
t_en: But... why? Why can't I get out still...

t_ch: 我不明白…你要「走」去哪里？
t_en: I don't get it... Where are you trying to go?

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不明白吗？但我也很难解释…要是他和姐姐，他们中有一个人在就好了……
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}, don't you get it? It's hard for me to explain... If only either he or my sister were here...

t_ch: 你只要跟着我就行了呀……
t_en: You just have to follow me...

t_ch: …不对，我不能从这里离开。他和姐姐，他们中只要有一个人在就好了…他们一定能解释清楚……
t_en: ...No, I can't leave this place. If only either he or my sister were here... they'd be able to explain everything to you...

t_ch: （这个小女孩说的话完全不符合逻辑…或许她遭受了什么刺激？）
t_en: (This girl is not making any sense at all... Perhaps she's been somehow traumatized?)

t_ch: （不能用太强硬的态度对待这孩子。慢慢来吧，先顺着她的话问下去。）
t_en: (Don't be too tough on this child. Be patient, and go along with what she's saying for now.)

t_ch: 能告诉我「他」的名字吗？
t_en: Can you tell me "his" name?

t_ch: 他…他是对希莉儿来说很重要的人。
t_en: He... he's a really really important person... to me.

t_ch: 大家都只喜欢姐姐…这也难怪，因为姐姐漂亮又聪明。只有他愿意和希莉儿玩…他觉得希莉儿很特别。
t_en: People only like my sister... That's no surprise since my sister is smart and pretty. All this time, he's the only one who's willing to play with me... He thinks I'm very special.

t_ch: 你的姐姐，她没陪着你吗？
t_en: Your sister... why isn't she with you?

t_ch: 姐姐她…她不知道去哪儿了。
t_en: She... she... I don't know where she is.

t_ch: 姐姐总是很忙，她身边总是围着很多人…我不认识的人。姐姐很会照顾人，也很温柔…可她一忙起来就没空管我了。
t_en: She's always so busy, and there're always so many people around her... people that I don't know. She is so caring and kind... but when she gets busy, she doesn't have time for me.

t_ch: 我只是想帮你，不用害怕。
t_en: I just want to help you. No need to get scared.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。也许只要再重复一次…希莉儿就能走出去了。
t_en: Thank you, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. Maybe if I try once more... I'll be able to get out.

t_ch: 呃…好，但怎么个重复法？
t_en: Hmm... okay. But how do we try once more?

t_ch: 可这里已经什么都没有了……
t_en: But there's nothing here anymore...

t_ch: 你看，这边……
t_en: Look! Over there...

t_ch: 你眨了一下眼，然后……
t_en: You blink, and then...

t_ch: 困住我的东西，总会不断地出现…它们不希望我走出去。是因为我犯了什么错吗？他说过，犯了错误就该道歉…可希莉儿该找谁道歉呢？
t_en: There's always something trapping me here... They don't want me to get out. Did I do something wrong? He said, "you should apologize if you do something wrong"... But who should I apologize to?

t_ch: 这些箱子到底是怎么回事？！
t_en: What on earth is going on with all these boxes!?

t_ch: 它们…总会不断地出现，不断逼着我思考。可我不是姐姐，我不擅长思考……
t_en: They... keep showing up and keep making me think and think. But I'm not like my sister, and I'm not a good thinker...

t_ch: 事情变得诡异起来了…
t_en: Things are starting to get weird...

t_ch: 这些箱子…总会不断地出现，不断逼着我思考。可我不是姐姐，我不够聪明…我不知道该怎么办。
t_en: These boxes... keep showing up and keep making me think and think. But I'm not like my sister, and not smart enough... and I don't know what to do.

t_ch: 我想让它们消失，我想离开这里…我想去见他，他答应希莉儿，会带我过上完美的生活……
t_en: I want them to disappear, and I wanna get out of here... I want to go and see him. He has promised to give me a perfect life...

t_ch: …我再帮你一次。
t_en: ...I'll help you again.

t_ch: 谢谢你，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。我就在这里等你。
t_en: Thank you {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. I'll wait here for you.

t_ch: 又解开了一个谜题，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}的脑子真好用！
t_en: That's another puzzle solved, {F#ma'am}{M#sir} — you're so smart!

t_ch: 不过…好像什么都没能改变呢。我还是被困在这里…走不出去。
t_en: But... nothing's changed though. I'm still stuck here... and can't get out.

t_ch: 你抓紧我就行，我带你出去。
t_en: Just hold on tight to me, and I'll get you out of here.

t_ch: …真的吗？你能带希莉儿离开这里？
t_en: ...Really? Can you really get me out of here?
Uh...

t_ch: （尝试抓住希莉儿的手）
t_en: (Try to grab onto Cyrille's hand)

t_ch: 你伸出手去，尝试握住女孩苍白、纤弱的腕关节。
t_en: You reach out and try to grab her pale, slender wrist.

t_ch: 但在接触到来前的一瞬间，一股巨大的斥力扇开了你的手掌——紧跟着的是一阵刺痛…酥麻，滚烫。
t_en: However, just before you reach her, a huge force repels your hand — followed by a tingling sensation, then a numbing burn.

t_ch: 某种力量在阻止你带走希莉儿…通过这一连串的反馈，它还完整地向你传达了另一则信息：停止尝试，否则等着你的会是更大的苦头。
t_en: A force is preventing you from taking Cyrille away... Based on this feedback, you understand it is trying to tell you to stop trying, or face more pain.

t_ch: 这是怎么回事…
t_en: What's going on...

t_ch: 欸？发生什么事了……
t_en: Huh? What happened...?

t_ch: 你的力气也太大了吧？
t_en: How are you this powerful?

t_ch: 欸…？你在说什么……
t_en: Huh...? What are you talking about...

t_ch: 果然，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}也帮不了我吗？哪怕找到了解开谜题的办法……
t_en: So, you can't help me either? Even if you've found a way to solve the puzzle...

t_ch: 没关系，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}不用难过，这不是你的错。我会在这里等到他来…他总是有办法解决困难。
t_en: That's okay, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. Don't be sad. It's not your fault. I'll wait for him to come back... He always knows how to fix things.

t_ch: 我去找帮手，你在这儿等着。
t_en: I'm going to find help. You wait here.

t_ch: …不必麻烦了，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。
t_en: ...Don't bother, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}.

t_ch: 让我再试一次。
t_en: Let me try again.

t_ch: …不用了，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。我不想让你这样的好心人受伤。
t_en: ...Don't worry, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. I don't want to hurt a kind person like you again.

t_ch: 我会接着努力，争取靠自己的力量走出去。他说过，只有努力的孩子才配得到表扬。
t_en: I'll keep on working hard and try to get out on my own. He said only hardworking kids deserve praise.

t_ch: 再见，{F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}。希望你在商店街逛得开心。
t_en: Bye-bye {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. Hope you have fun on the shopping street.

t_ch: （那个孩子…还有那些不断出现的军械箱…究竟是怎么回事？）
t_en: (That girl... and those Armory Boxes that kept showing up... What the heck is going on here?)

t_ch: （或许是裂界产生的某种现实扭曲现象？但那个女孩看上去有血有肉，不像幻觉……）
t_en: (Maybe it's some kind of reality distortion in the Fragmentum? But that kid was of flesh and blood, and not some kind of hallucination...)

t_ch: （只能去找人帮忙了。对这种奇异事件有了解的人…只能想到他了。）
t_en: (Got to find someone to help. Someone who understands this weird stuff... and I can only think of him.)

t_ch: 嗯哼…嗯哼……
t_en: Mhm... mhm...

t_ch: 哎哟，这回真让我撞上大运了！机械臂小子这回居然不参赛，嘿嘿，那操作空间可就大了……
t_en: Wow, I sure hit the jackpot this time around! That guy with the robot arm isn't competing — yes! That's so much more wiggle room...

t_ch: 你在那儿神神叨叨什么呢？
t_en: What are you babbling on about there?

t_ch: 什么操作空间？
t_en: What wiggle room?

t_ch: …哎哟喂，别这么吓我啊！老桑博这小心脏脆弱的很呐……
t_en: ...Argh, don't scare me like that! This ol' heart of mine is really weak, y'know...

t_ch: 咳咳，别在意…你懂我的，无非就是生意上的事儿嘛！这回保证和你们没关系。
t_en: Ahem, don't worry... You know me, it's just business! This time I promise it's got nothing to do with you guys.

t_ch: 话说回来，不是还有那么句话吗？「无事不登三宝殿」…你这回主动跑来跟我搭讪，想必是有什么要紧事吧？
t_en: Anyway... To what do I owe the pleasure, this time? You've gone through all the trouble to find me here, so you must have something urgent, right?

t_ch: 人命关天…
t_en: It's a matter of life and death...

t_ch: …咋了，有人受伤了？那不是该去找娜塔莎吗？
t_en: ...What? Is someone getting seriously injured? Shouldn't you ask Natasha for help instead?

t_ch: 出了这么大的事儿，你第一个想到的居然是来找我…这就是坚不可摧的友谊啊！说吧朋友，只要是老桑博能力之内的事，绝对帮你安排到位！
t_en: Something this big happens, and your first thought is to find... me? Now that's what I call an ironclad friendship! Tell me, my dear old friend — I'll do anything for you as long as it's within my capabilities!

t_ch: 这事说来有点不可思议…
t_en: Well, actually, this matter is a bit strange...

t_ch: …怎么，还有你没见识过的怪事？
t_en: ...Huh? Is there really something out there that even you've never come across before?

t_ch: 然后你第一个想到的居然是来找我…这就是坚不可摧的友谊啊！说吧朋友，只要是老桑博能力之内的事，绝对帮你安排到位！
t_en: And your first thought is to find... me? Now that's what I call an ironclad friendship! Tell me, my dear old friend — I'll do anything for you as long as it's within my capabilities!

t_ch: 把你在边缘通路偶遇希莉儿的事告诉了桑博……
t_en: You tell Sampo about your chance encounter with Cyrille at Backwater Pass...

t_ch: 呃…你确定那不是失眠导致的幻觉？
t_en: Oh... Are you sure it wasn't some kind of hallucination induced by sleep deprivation?

t_ch: 那什么，这事…怎么说呢，咱们要不就别追究了吧？你要是睡眠质量不行的话，可以去娜塔莎那儿整点补剂……
t_en: So uh, this thing... ah, how about — let's not look into it any further? If you're having problems sleeping, then Natasha will have something for you...

t_ch: 你已经答应帮我了，不许反悔。
t_en: You've promised to help me. Don't go back on your word.

t_ch: 这…唉，我这一时脑热……
t_en: This... ah, I'm just saying stuff on impulse...

t_ch: 你是不是觉得这事没赚头？
t_en: Are you worried that it's not profitable?

t_ch: 啊？当、当然不是，你可别把我当成唯利是图的主啊……
t_en: Mmm? O—of course not. Don't paint me as such a greedy mercenary...

t_ch: 嗐，谁叫干咱这行最重要的就是客户信用呢…行吧，那我就陪你过去看看。
t_en: Hey, the most important thing in this line of business is the customer's trust... alright. I'll go with you and take a look.

t_ch: ——你确定不是出现幻觉了吗？我和你说，娜塔莎那儿什么药都有，不止能治失眠……
t_en: Are you sure you weren't hallucinating? I'm telling you, Natasha has all kinds of medicines for you. Anything!

t_ch: 别贫嘴了…
t_en: Stop wasting your breath...

t_ch: 你别让我后悔…
t_en: Don't let me down...

t_ch: 呃…行，知道了，不说了！咱们走着。
t_en: Um... Okay, I got it! Enough chitchat. Let's go.
Mmm...

t_ch: （继续保持沉默）
t_en: (Keep silent)

t_ch: 那什么…你倒是说句话啊？
t_en: Can you, like, say something?

t_ch: 我就说吧——你肯定是睡眠质量太差导致出现幻觉了！
t_en: Well, then, I will say it out loud for you. You are definitely suffering from hallucinations due to a lack of sleep!

t_ch: 难道真的是失眠…
t_en: Could it really be insomnia...?

t_ch: 我就说吧，你桑博哥哥的判断一向准确！
t_en: I told you so. My diagnoses have always been accurate!

t_ch: 所以…呃，根本没有什么小女孩，也没有什么军械箱。那些东西都是你凭空想象出来的，对吧？
t_en: So... hmm, there's no little girl to begin with. And no Armory Boxes, either. You just made everything up out of thin air, right?

t_ch: 不过…考虑到这里是裂界，这事儿也不能完全怪你。这破地方就是这么离奇——不瞒你说，我也在裂界里见过各种奇异现象，等你跟我一样习惯了以后，就不会一惊一乍了。
t_en: But... since we're affected by the Fragmentum here, you're not completely to blame. It's just how weird this place is — and to tell you the truth, I've seen all kinds of strange stuff in the Fragmentum. You'll get used to it soon enough.

t_ch: …那个，你要是没有别的要紧事，我就回磐岩镇去了？我跟老史考特还有生意要——嗯？这是什么？
t_en: ...Well, if there's nothing else going on, I'll be heading back to Boulder Town. I've still got some unfinished business with Scott. Wait... eh? What's this?

t_ch: …这是啥？看着像是什么人留下的笔记？似乎有点年头了。
t_en: ...What's this? Someone left some notes behind. They look old.

t_ch: 看不出有什么特别的…也没有藏宝图或者暗语之类的记号，好像没啥价值。你要是觉得有点意思，不如就自己留着吧？
t_en: I don't see anything special... There's no treasure map or secret code words. Nothing of value. You can hold onto this if you want.

t_ch: 呃，那我就先走一步了，大客户！希望你能改善改善睡眠质量，下次找我的时候清醒一点儿……
t_en: Mmm, then I'll be heading out, my valued patron! Here's hoping your insomnia improves. Make sure you're fully awake next time you come and see me!

t_ch: {F#姐姐}{M#哥哥}还不走吗？没事的，不用担心我……
t_en: {F#Ma'am}{M#Sir}, you're still here? All good, don't worry about me...

t_ch: 我会努力，争取靠自己的力量走出这里……
t_en: I'll work hard and try to get out on my own...

t_ch: 我们就从这里开始搜索茉莉小姐的东西吧，没必要沿着阶梯再向上了。那里只有茫茫无际的战场遗址，不属于旧城塞的范围。
t_en: Let's start searching for Miss Molly's things from here. We don't need to go up the stairs. That place is not part of the old city fortress and has nothing but ancient battlefields.

t_ch: 虽然我们来时没遇到什么敌人，但回头就不好说了。眼睛放亮点——我感觉这里今天的气氛有点不同寻常。
t_en: Thankfully, we didn't encounter any enemies coming here, but I can't promise it'll stay that way on our way back. Keep your eyes peeled. This place gives off a strange vibe today.

t_ch: 你好像对这里很熟悉？
t_en: You seem familiar with this place?

t_ch: 是啊。我之前经常陪同杰帕德来这里勘察地形——不过那是很久以前的事了。
t_en: Yeah. I used to come here with Gepard to survey the terrain — but that was a long time ago.

t_ch: 戍卫官自不必说，亲自测绘战场对情报官来说也是相当重要的。可惜前线压力越来越大，我们不得已退守铁卫禁区…这些情报最后都没怎么派上用场。
t_en: Needless to say, it was very important for an intelligence officer to personally map the battlefield. Unfortunately, the pressure on the front line increased so quickly that we had to retreat to the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone... And those pieces of intelligence were not very useful in the end.

t_ch: 气氛有什么不同吗？
t_en: What's so strange about this place's vibe?

t_ch: 太…冷清了。要是平时的话，我们根本不可能在避免战斗的情况下一路走到这里。
t_en: It's... too quiet. Normally, we couldn't have come all this way without any fights.

t_ch: 从结果来看这当然是好事…但我总本能觉得有点不对劲。总之，我们接下来多加小心吧。
t_en: That may seem like a good thing... But deep down, I'm disturbed by this strange quietness. Anyway, let's be more careful from now on.

t_ch: 我们为什么要从这里开始搜索？
t_en: Why should we start searching here?

t_ch: 这个旧城塞的构造是南北走向，而我们所在的位置是它的最南边——
t_en: The old citadel runs from the north to the south, and we are now at the southernmost end—

t_ch: 从这里开始搜索就不必多走回头路。不过从北边开始搜索也一样，我只是来时随便找了个方向罢了。
t_en: If we start from here, we can save a trip going backward. Technically, it'd be the same if we were to start from the north. I just picked this direction at random when we came.

t_ch: 好，我们出发吧。
t_en: Okay, let's go.

t_ch: 嗯，我们走。虽然我们来时没有遇到任何敌人，但后面就不好说了。如果一定要开战的话，我也会从旁援护你。
t_en: Great, let's go. Even though we didn't encounter any enemies coming here, it's still hard to say what will happen later. If we get into a fight, I will cover you.

t_ch: 如果你不幸战败，我当然会想办法带你撤退到安全地带，等你准备好后再一起继续探索。
t_en: If it so happens that you are defeated, I will definitely fight my way to bring you back to a safe place and continue our exploration when you are ready.

t_ch: 但最好别让这种事发生，毕竟我能提供的支援也是有限度的…拜托你了。
t_en: But it's best to avoid that. After all, there is only so much help I can give you... So stay sharp, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 哔嘟！哔嘟！发现近似物件，发现近似物件——请问是否要切换至搜索模式？
t_en: Beep! Beep! Matching object found, matching object found — do you want to switch to search mode?

t_ch: …什么声音？
t_en: ...What's that sound?

t_ch: 呃，{NICKNAME}…你怎么随身带着这种东西？
t_en: Um, {NICKNAME}, why are you carrying that thing with you?

t_ch: 哔嘟…查宝不是东西。本机为「家用寻物仪『理查德』•版本<unbreak>0.63</unbreak>•专家型•年度豪华版」，昵称「查宝」。
t_en: Beep... Findie is not a thing. I am Richard, the Home-Use Object Finder. Model information: Version <unbreak>0.63</unbreak>, Professional model, Annual Deluxe Edition. Nickname: "Findie."

t_ch: 它能帮我们找东西，挺好用的。
t_en: It can help us find things. Pretty useful.

t_ch: 原来如此…你们「外来者」倒是有不少有用的小玩意呢。
t_en: I see... You outsiders sure have a lot of gadgets that come in handy.

t_ch: 它声称自己可以找东西…
t_en: It claims it can find things.

t_ch: 呃…这是说它不太可靠吗？但它好像确实发现了什么东西……
t_en: Uh... Does that mean it's not very reliable? But it does seem to have found something...

t_ch: 不管了，多一双眼睛也不是坏事。我们试试看吧。
t_en: Anyway, one more pair of eyes might not be a bad thing. Let's try it out.

t_ch: 它是我的情感支持机器人。
t_en: It's my emotional support robot.

t_ch: …你说是那就是。但它刚才好像自称「家用寻物仪」——这是说，它能帮我们找东西吗？
t_en: ...If you say so. But it just called itself a "Home-Use Object Finder." So, can it help us find things?

t_ch: 不管了，多一双眼睛也不是坏事。我们试试看吧。
t_en: Anyway, one more pair of eyes might not be a bad thing. Let's try it out.

t_ch: 喂，查宝！你可以为我们带路吗？
t_en: Hey, Findie! Can you show us the way?

t_ch: 对对对，太对了。需要找东西的时候，呼唤查宝就对了。
t_en: Yes, yes, yes. When you need to find something, just call Findie.

t_ch: 当前要目标物件为「被藏起来的东西」，范围内共「一个」目标物件。请根据「家用寻物仪安全使用指南」，跟随本机进行搜索。
t_en: The current target is "something hidden." And there is "one" such target in detectable range. Please follow yours truly to conduct the search in accordance with the "Home-Use Object Finder Safety Manual."

t_ch: 它好像很有信心的样子…那我们就跟着它行动吧。
t_en: It seems confident... Let's follow it.

t_ch: 嗯？这里好像什么也没有啊……
t_en: Huh? Looks like there's nothing here...

t_ch: 啊，不对。{NICKNAME}，你看这里：这块地砖有点不对劲。上面好像刻了一行字呢。
t_en: Hmm, wait a minute. {NICKNAME}, look, there's something wrong with this floor tile. There seems to be a line of words engraved on it.

t_ch: 「踩我」…这是要我们踩这块地砖的意思吗？
t_en: "Stomp on me"... Does that mean we should step on this floor tile?

t_ch: 难道有什么东西被埋在下面了？
t_en: Maybe something is buried underneath?

t_ch: 我想不是。如果是埋在下面的话，这里不应该写「把我挖开」吗？
t_en: I don't think so. If it's buried underneath, shouldn't it say "dig me up"?

t_ch: 既然它写的是「踩我」…那我们就按它说的做吧。
t_en: Well, it says "stomp on me"... So, let's do just that.

t_ch: 难道这是什么压力机关踏板？
t_en: Is this some kind of pedal trigger?

t_ch: 我想这样比较合理。既然如此，那我们就按它说的做吧。
t_en: That makes more sense. In that case, let's do as it says.

t_ch: （狠狠跺一脚）
t_en: (Stomp hard)
...Huh?

t_ch: 哇，你看，前面这块地砖浮出来了——这是什么，暗格吗？里面还有枚椭圆形的……
t_en: Wow, look, the tile up ahead is floating — what's this, a secret compartment? There is also an oval...thingy...?

t_ch: 一枚小巧的卵形相框中载着略微泛黄的肖像，静静地躺在地砖改装成的暗格中。
t_en: A small oval frame containing a slightly yellowish portrait lies quietly in a secret compartment hidden in the tile.

t_ch: 将肖像翻转，你们看到一<color=#dbc291ff>「顺时针旋转箭头」</color>的图案，墨迹已然干涸。
t_en: You flipped the portrait over to its back side and see a pattern of <color=#dbc291ff>a clockwise rotating arrow</color>, and the ink has dried up.

t_ch: 历代情报官的照片都挂在我办公室的墙上。如果只看这幅肖像，这位老巴拉金还是很有风度的…说是帅哥也不为过。
t_en: I have photos of all the intelligence officers throughout the ages hanging up on my office wall. Compared with them, old Balakin here does look quite classy in this portrait... One can even describe him as handsome.

t_ch: 现在的话…我只感觉好像有某种珍贵的东西从内心中残酷地消失了……
t_en: Alas, now I just feel like something precious has been cruelly ripped from my heart...

t_ch: 有些事，不知道会活得更幸福。
t_en: Ignorance is bliss.

t_ch: …不要这么冷漠地复述悲惨的现实啊。
t_en: Now you are making me all sad.

t_ch: 这可能就是静态美人吧…
t_en: This might be the so-called "Static Beauty."

t_ch: 是啊，但我对这种属性确实没感觉……
t_en: Maybe, but I'm kinda agnostic about this.

t_ch: 不也挺好的吗？
t_en: Isn't that great?

t_ch: …你一点都不明白！
t_en: You just don't get it!

t_ch: 冷静，佩拉，冷静…快把今天看到的都忘掉……
t_en: Calm down, Pela, calm down... Forget what you saw today...

t_ch: ——好，我已经全部忘光光了。哇，{NICKNAME}，你快看：巴拉金还在这幅肖像背后画了个<color=#dbc291ff>「顺时针旋转箭头」</color>的图案呢！
t_en: ...Okay, I've put all that behind me now. Wow, {NICKNAME}, look! Balakin has also painted a pattern of <color=#dbc291ff>a clockwise rotation arrow</color> on the back of this portrait!

t_ch: 这可能就是新的线索。
t_en: This may be a new clue.

t_ch: 既然这是老巴拉金留下来的东西，还用这种隐蔽的机关保护着，那大概就是新的线索了。可他到底是想表达什么呢？
t_en: Since this is something left by old Balakin and protected by this hidden mechanism, it very much could be a new clue. But what is he trying to say?

t_ch: 感觉…毫无头绪。我们还是跟着查宝接着找其他线索吧。
t_en: Ugh... I don't have any ideas. Let's just follow Findie for now and look for other clues.

t_ch: 哇，一个箭头！好神奇呀！
t_en: Wow, an arrow! How amazing!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你可以不用陪着我一起演的。这样会显得我很蠢……
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you don't have to patronize me like that. It's making me feel stupid.

t_ch: 总之，这个箭头图案应该就是进一步的线索——只是我也不太清楚这意味着什么…我们还是跟着查宝接着找其他线索吧。
t_en: Anyway, this arrow pattern must be another clue — but I'm not sure what this means... Let's follow Findie and look for other clues.

t_ch: 不要再故作坚强了，佩拉小姐…
t_en: No need to put on a happy face, Miss Pela.

t_ch: …住口！我已经全都忘掉了，我什么都不知道…不要再让我想起来那种事了！
t_en: ... Shut up. I've put it all behind me now. I don't know anything... Don't you even try to remind me of that kind of thing!

t_ch: 总之，这个箭头图案应该就是进一步的线索——只是我也不太清楚这意味着什么…我们还是跟着查宝接着找其他线索吧。
t_en: Anyway, this arrow pattern must be another clue — but I'm not sure what this means... Let's follow Findie and look for other clues.

t_ch: 这次的线索倒是很直白嘛——你看这块地砖上，刻了一个<color=#dbc291ff>「逆时针旋转箭头」</color>的图案…想必和之前的线索有所关联吧。
t_en: The clue this time is very straightforward — you see, on this floor tile, there is a pattern of <color=#dbc291ff>a counterclockwise rotation arrow</color>... It must be somehow related to the previous clue.

t_ch: 查宝到底是怎么找到这东西的？
t_en: How on earth did Findie find this thing?

t_ch: 嗯？让我看看……
t_en: Huh? Let me see...

t_ch: …果然。这块地砖没被砌进地里，也是人为改装过的、暗格一类的东西。查宝大概是因为这个把它判定成「被藏起来的东西」了吧。
t_en: ... Just as I thought. This floor tile was not built into the ground. It was also artificially modified and concealed. It's probably because of this that Findie judged it as "something hidden."

t_ch: 这些箭头到底是什么意思？
t_en: What exactly do these arrows mean?

t_ch: 实话说，我目前还不能确凿地指出这些图案到底是什么意思，但这大概和某种<color=#dbc291ff>「可旋转的构造」</color>有关。
t_en: To be honest, I don't know for sure, but it's probably related to some kind of <color=#dbc291ff>rotatable structure</color>.

t_ch: 总之，我先把它记录下来，然后也给你抄一份。喏，给你。
t_en: Anyway, I'll write it down first, and then I'll copy it for you. Here you are.

t_ch: 佩拉递来的纸张上依次画着<color=#dbc291ff>「顺时针旋转箭头」</color>和<color=#dbc291ff>「逆时针旋转箭头」</color>的图案，底下还附有她关于「可旋转的构造」批注。
t_en: On the sheet of paper Pela gave you, there are several ordered drawings of <color=#dbc291ff>clockwise rotation arrow</color> and <color=#dbc291ff>counterclockwise rotation arrow</color>, with her annotation at the bottom that says "rotatable structure."

t_ch: 将纸张翻面，你看到一些字迹娟丽却略显潦草的手稿。词句支离破碎，你只能大约猜测出这段文字与名为「阿尔乔姆」、「安娜」和「达米尔」的人有关。
t_en: You turn the sheet over and see some beautifully written but illegible scribbles. The words are all jumbled, but you do see that this passage is about three characters, respectively named Artem, Anna and Dalmir.

t_ch: 谢谢，我们接着出发吧。
t_en: Thank you. Let's keep going.

t_ch: 好，我们出发……
t_en: Okay, let's go...

t_ch: 那个…这段手稿是什么？
t_en: So... What are these texts?

t_ch: 嗯？不就是刚才那些线索……
t_en: Huh? The clues we just saw...

t_ch: …等等，不对，我忘了！你、你先把那张纸还给我…！
t_en: ... Wait, no, I forgot! Give me back that piece of paper now!

t_ch: 好吧，给你。
t_en: Okay, here you are.

t_ch: 哼，算你识相……
t_en: Humph, good...

t_ch: 我要珍藏佩拉小姐的大作…
t_en: I want to treasure Miss Pela's masterpiece...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你，啊…气死我了！你不给我我就要抢了！给我拿来——
t_en: {NICKNAME}, ahhhh... You gotta be kidding me! If you don't give it back to me right now, I'll... I'll have to take it back! Give it to me.

t_ch: 呼…让我重、重新找一张空白页……
t_en: Grr... Let me find another blank page to write it down...

t_ch: …好了，给你。记得把刚才看到的东西统统忘掉，也不许和别人说起这件事！
t_en: ...Done. Take this one instead. Forget all the texts you saw on the back, and don't you dare tell anyone else about it!

t_ch: 我们到了——从这里沿左侧道路向前就是堡垒防线的后方「筑城领域」，右边则可以取道登上锋线堡垒的城垛。
t_en: Here we are. Now, on the left, it's a road towards the Fortified Zone, which is just behind the fortress defense line. And on the right, it's the path leading to the Frontline Fortress's parapet.

t_ch: 我们先分开行动吧。筑城领域中有着和铁卫禁区中同样构造的旋转栈桥，我得去调查一下它的控制终端。
t_en: Let's split up for now. In the Fortified Zone, there is a revolving gear bridge that has the same structure as the one in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone. I have to go there to investigate its control terminal.

t_ch: 我会顺着左侧道路继续前进，在这期间就拜托你去右侧的锋线堡垒寻找线索了。
t_en: So, I'll pick the road on the left. In the meantime, please use the path on the right to go to the Frontline Fortress and look for clues there.

t_ch: 你要是遇到裂界怪物怎么办？
t_en: What if you run into a Fragmentum monster?

t_ch: 来时我已经确认过了，那条道路上堆满了路障和杂物，怪物是不会靠近那里的。
t_en: On the way here, I checked and found that that road is full of roadblocks and debris. Monsters wouldn't get close to where I'm going.

t_ch: 虽然很不想承认…但以我的身型来说，穿越那些障碍绰绰有余。所以就让我一个人去吧。
t_en: Plus, all those obstacles are much easier for someone as small as me to pass through, even though I hate to talk about my size. Anyway, I can do it alone.

t_ch: 栈桥四周不都有控制终端吗？
t_en: Aren't there control terminals all around the gear bridge?

t_ch: 这种旋转栈桥在设计初期都会配置一个数据储存端，只有通过这个端口才能查看和调用旋转栈桥的各种数据……
t_en: This kind of gear bridge is pre-equipped with a data storage terminal. Only through this very specific terminal can you view essential data about the gear bridge.

t_ch: 我需要有些想法需要验证，所以必须去调查那个数据储存端——从这里沿左边道路往下就是了。
t_en: I need to verify some theories of mine, so I have to investigate this data terminal myself, which is just down the road on the left.

t_ch: 明白了，你要注意安全。
t_en: I see. Stay safe.

t_ch: 哼哼，可不要小看情报官——我已经计算到各种可能性啦，肯定不会出事的。
t_en: Humph, never underestimate an intelligence officer — I've worked out every possible scenario, and nothing will go wrong.

t_ch: 那我们就分头行动吧。各自的调查完成之后，我们就在大桥另一边会合。
t_en: So let's split up. After we finish our respective investigations, we shall meet on the other side of the bridge.

t_ch: 毫不意外地，此处的地砖也被刻上了<color=#dbc291ff>「顺时针旋转箭头」</color>的图案。
t_en: Unsurprisingly, the tiles here are also engraved with the pattern of <color=#dbc291ff>a clockwise rotation arrow</color>.

t_ch: （记录在佩拉的手记上吧。）
t_en: (Write it down in Pela's notebook.)

t_ch: 你尝试依旧法操作旋转栈桥的控制终端，但没能成功。
t_en: You tried operating the control terminal of the gear bridge, but failed.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}——能听到吗——{NICKNAME}——！
t_en: {NICKNAME}! Can you hear me? {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 佩拉——我在这里——
t_en: Pela, I'm here!

t_ch: 控制终端——好像坏了——
t_en: The control terminal... seems to be broken...

t_ch: 旋转栈桥——被锁定了——
t_en: The gear bridge... it's locked...

t_ch: 想办法——解锁——！
t_en: Find a way... to unlock it!

t_ch: 我想想——你等着——
t_en: Let me see. Hold on!

t_ch: 我该怎么解锁——？！
t_en: How do I unlock it!?

t_ch: 记得——笔记——顺序——！
t_en: Remember the sequence from the notes!

t_ch: （想想办法，试着解锁操作终端吧……）
t_en: (Let's try and find a way to unlock the terminal...)

t_ch: 顺时针旋转箭头
t_en: Clockwise rotation arrow

t_ch: 逆时针旋转箭头
t_en: Counterclockwise rotation arrow

t_ch: 操作终端内传来清脆的响声。
t_en: You hear a clear sound coming from the control terminal.

t_ch: （看来这步是正确的……）
t_en: (This seems to be the correct step...)

t_ch: 操作终端内传来一连串的闷响，似乎是复位了。
t_en: You hear a series of dull noises coming from the control terminal. It seems to have been reset.

t_ch: （看来这步是错误的…重新开始操作吧。）
t_en: (This seems to be the wrong way... Needs a restart.)

t_ch: 操作终端内的机械结构不断转动着。随后，一声铿然清亮的脆响传来，操作终端重新归于寂静。
t_en: The mechanical structure in the control terminal is rotating nonstop. Then, you hear a loud and clear sound coming from within, and the terminal becomes silent again.

t_ch: （似乎可以控制栈桥旋转了。）
t_en: (The rotation of the gear bridge seems to be operable again.)

t_ch: 啊，终于！我还以为你过不来了……
t_en: Ah, finally! I almost began to think you couldn't make it.

t_ch: 小菜一碟。
t_en: A piece of cake.

t_ch: 你解决得倒也挺轻巧嘛，不愧是和前任大守护者斗智斗勇的人。
t_en: You handled that with such elegance. Looks like you truly are worth your salt.

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you all right?

t_ch: 谢谢你关心我，不过我没事——毕竟我可是银鬃铁卫的情报官。
t_en: Thank you for your concern, but I'm fine. I'm an intelligence officer of the Silvermane Guards, after all.

t_ch: 你对我就这么没信心吗…
t_en: You thought I couldn't do it?

t_ch: 哼哼，开个玩笑而已。
t_en: Haha, I was just kidding.

t_ch: 来梳理一下刚才发生的事吧——我检查过数据储存端后，发现只要按照图案给定的顺序操作栈桥，就可以解除栈桥的制动。
t_en: Let's recount what just happened. After checking the data storage terminal, I found that the gear bridge brakes can be released by following the sequence of these given patterns.

t_ch: 不过，我还是不太明白老巴拉金为什么要留下这么一套密码，就想着先和你会合，过后一起讨论……
t_en: However, I still don't quite understand why old Balakin left these passwords behind. So I thought I should meet up with you first and discuss it together.

t_ch: 谁能想到——我刚一过桥，旋转栈桥居然自己转了起来，把我留在了另一边！
t_en: But who would have thought... The moment I crossed the gear bridge, it started rotating by itself, leaving me on the other side!

t_ch: 我试图操作机关，结果发现栈桥居然因为紧急制动抱死了——
t_en: I tried to control that thing, but the bridge was locked by an emergency brake.

t_ch: 我实在没办法，情急之下只好大声喊你名字…还好你听到了，谢天谢地。
t_en: There was nothing I could do. In a hurry, I called out your name... Fortunately, you heard me. Thank god.

t_ch: 你应该知道如何解除制动吧？
t_en: I suppose you know how to release the brakes?

t_ch: 实话说，在调查数据储存端之前，我真不知道有这种操作…铁卫禁区栈桥的制动系统是希露瓦他们改装过的，用起来比这个方便多了。
t_en: To be honest, before looking at the data terminal, I really didn't know it could work this way... The braking system of the bridge in the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone was modified by Serval, and is much easier to use.

t_ch: 而且，最重要的问题是——我按照终端里的指令试着操作了一番，结果旋转栈桥纹丝不动。
t_en: Anyway, the strange thing is... I tried operating the terminal according to the instructions, and the gear bridge didn't move at all.

t_ch: 我猜应该是老巴拉金亲自修改了操作指令吧，而且还用旧指令伪装起来了。要不是你找到了正确的指令，我还真有可能要一辈子待在这里了。谢谢你……
t_en: I guess old Balakin somehow modified the instructions himself and purposefully used some outdated instructions as a ruse. If it weren't for you, I would have been stuck here forever. So, I really can't thank you enough.

t_ch: 为什么旋转栈桥自己动起来了？
t_en: Why did the gear bridge move on its own?

t_ch: 是我大意了…我没留意地上居然还有个压力机关踏板。
t_en: I was careless... I didn't notice the pressure pedal on the ground.

t_ch: 要是我当时带着查宝，大概就不会发生这种事了…不过这样的话，你也找不到最后的线索了吧？真麻烦啊。
t_en: If I had brought Findie, it probably wouldn't have happened... But then you probably wouldn't be able to find the last clue. What a hassle.

t_ch: 你这样喊，不怕引来怪物吗？
t_en: Aren't you afraid that your shouting will attract monsters?

t_ch: 我也考虑过这个问题，但那时除了喊也没其他办法了。好在它们并没有被我的喊声引过来，就结果而言还是没问题的。
t_en: I was aware of this, but there was simply no other way at that time. Lucky for us, they didn't come.

t_ch: 差点让你一个人留在这里了…
t_en: Almost left you here alone...

t_ch: 你这不是来了嘛，皆大欢喜。这附近我也仔细调查过了，茉莉小姐所说的「钥匙」就在这里——
t_en: But you came to help in the end and now all is well. By the way, I also found the "key" Miss Molly talked about. It's here.

t_ch: 只是，似乎有个看起来很凶悍的怪物正盘踞在那附近…我一个人没办法对付它，所以也只能在原地等你过来了。
t_en: Also, there seems to be a scary monster nearby... I wouldn't be able to deal with it alone, so I have to wait for your help.

t_ch: 那我们上吧，打倒它！
t_en: Then let's go kick its butt!

t_ch: 谢天谢地，总算到头了…
t_en: Thank God, it's over...

t_ch: 是啊，我腿都走酸了…赶紧拿到「钥匙」就回去吧！
t_en: Hear, hear. My legs are sore... Grab the key and let's go back!

t_ch: 我会在旁边援护你的，放手干吧。加油！
t_en: I'll cover for you. Let's go. Good luck!

t_ch: 这个钻头样的首饰，上面刻着「巴拉金」的字样…应该就是茉莉小姐要找的「钥匙」了。
t_en: This piece of drill-shaped jewelry is engraved with the word "Balakin"... It must be the key Miss Molly is looking for.

t_ch: 任务完成，我们回机械屋去吧。这一趟可真够折腾的……
t_en: Mission accomplished. Let's head back to the workshop. This trip has been so tiring.

t_ch: 那我们就先沿着来时的路开始跟着查宝一起搜索吧。出发！
t_en: Let's use Findie to search along the way we came. Let's go!

t_ch: 好像有怪物来到这附近了…是我们动静太大了吗？
t_en: Looks like monsters are here now... Were we too loud?

t_ch: 我们还是尽量避免战斗，小心前进吧。
t_en: Let's avoid fighting them and move forward with care.

t_ch: 这个敌人盘踞在道路上…只有打倒它才能前进了。
t_en: The enemy is guarding this path... Looks like we have to defeat them to go forward.

t_ch: 哔嘟！哔嘟！发现近似物件，发现近似物件——请问是否要切换至搜索模式？
t_en: Beep! Beep! Matching object found, matching object found — do you want to switch to search mode?

t_ch: 查宝好像有反应了，在这附近仔细搜索吧。
t_en: Findie seems to have found something. Let's take a closer look here.

t_ch: 游荡的怪物来到这边了…不要惊动他们，快速通过这里吧。
t_en: Some roaming monsters are here... Try not to alert them. Let's get past this area quickly.

t_ch: 这个怪物似乎没有离开大门附近的意思…只能打倒它了。
t_en: This monster doesn't seem to have any intention of leaving the area near the gate... Guess we'll have to take it out.

t_ch: （似乎听到了旋转栈桥运作时的轰鸣……）
t_en: (I seem to hear a gear bridge moving...)
